title: Radiology department — 1 week
description: 15 doctors across junior / senior / consultant. Clean slate. Solves to OPTIMAL in seconds — great smoke test.

title: Busy hospital — 2 weeks with leave
description: 22 doctors, a public holiday, and a handful of leave blocks. Realistic mid-sized problem; shows how leave affects coverage.

title: Nursing ward — 2 weeks
description: 17 nurses across ward / senior / manager tiers with Medical and Surgical sub-wards. Same engine, different vocabulary.
highlights: Tiers: Staff Nurse / Senior Nurse / Ward Manager

title: NSPLib-shaped · n30 × d7
description: 30-doctor / 7-day roster in the parameter envelope of NSPLib's classic Vanhoucke & Maenhout (2007) benchmark family. Three 'skill classes' (our tiers), realistic leave, preferred-shift requests. Run this through /lab/benchmark to see CP-SAT vs baselines on an NRP-literature-sized problem.
benchmark_caveat: Shaped to match the n30/d7/s3 parameter envelope. Not a 1:1 import of a specific NSPLib instance — see docs/BRIEFING_2026-04-23.md §4.1 for the adapter follow-up.

title: Curtois-shaped · BCV 4-week
description: 30-doctor / 4-week roster in the parameter envelope of Curtois's BCV (Belgian Children's Valentine) family from U. Nottingham's NRP collection. Mixed leave + call blocks + soft preferences + one part-timer (0.5 FTE) + one max-oncall cap. The stress-test scenario for fairness and coverage.
benchmark_caveat: Shaped to match the BCV family's parameter envelope. Not a 1:1 import of a specific Curtois instance — see docs/BRIEFING_2026-04-23.md §4.1 for the adapter follow-up.
